About Samotics

Monitoring the assets no other system can reach.

Samotics monitors the industrial assets no other system can reach. Founded in 2015 to solve a specific problem: 30 to 40% of critical rotating assets have no viable monitoring path. We built Electrical Signature Analysis from scratch to cover them.

Today, SAM4 runs across 10,000+ assets on five continents. Every alert is reviewed by a reliability engineer before it reaches your team.

Talk to Us See our timeline ↓
10,000+
assets monitored
90.8%
fault detection accuracy
80+
customers worldwide
5
continents
€80M+
detection value identified
Our Story

How SAM4 started

Started with vibration

Founded in Leiden to apply AI to industrial machine data. Built our own sensor system. Started with vibration, the established standard.

Discovered the gap

Vibration sensors need mounting on the machine. For submerged pumps, hazardous zones, and remote sites, that's not possible. 30 to 40% of critical assets had no monitoring path.

Built ESA from scratch

Developed a system that reads the electrical signature at the motor control cabinet. After years of iteration and field validation, SAM4 launched. It covers the assets vibration couldn't reach.

Strategic Partnerships

Backed by companies that define industrial automation

ABB, SLB, and the EIB don't invest in technologies they expect to commoditise. Their backing reflects a specific bet: that ESA covers assets no other approach can reach, at a scale no startup competitor can match.

ABB

10% Equity Partnership

SAM4 ESA integrated into ABB's flagship ACS880 variable frequency drives. ABB account teams introduce Samotics to enterprise customers globally.

ABB partnership details →

SLB

Strategic Partnership

Strategic partnership with Schlumberger. Joint deployment on upstream oil and gas assets. SLB's global service network extends SAM4's reach into the most demanding industrial environments.

European Investment Bank

€20M Financing

EIB backing signals technology maturity and commercial viability at the institutional level. Unlocking growth across Europe and beyond.

Interested in partnering? See our partner programme →

Leadership

Samotics leadership team

Jasper Hoogeweegen

co-CEO

Leads strategy and investor relations. Former management consulting at McKinsey.

Simon Jagers

co-CEO & Founder

Leads commercial growth and strategic partnerships. Former enterprise tech at Oracle and EMC.

Gerben Gooijers

CTO & Founder

Built the ESA detection engine and SAM4 hardware platform from scratch.

Erik Gaarenstroom

COO

Runs operations, delivery, and customer success across five continents.

Hans van den Heuvel

CFO

Manages finance, governance, and investor relations for a scaling enterprise.

Thijs Bootsma

CPO

Leads product strategy, roadmap, and the SAM4 platform experience.

Advisors

Jeroen van der Veer

Former CEO of Royal Dutch Shell. Former Chairman of Philips. Provides strategic counsel on energy sector positioning and enterprise governance.

Dr. Johan Paulides

World-leading researcher in electric motor technology at AE Magnetics. Provides technical advisory on ESA signal physics and fault detection methodology.

Working at Samotics

Dutch. Direct. Collaborative.

The best ideas win here, regardless of who has them. We're approximately 103 people solving a hard technical problem: building the hardware, the AI, and the domain expertise to monitor equipment nobody else can reach.

Real problems, real impact

The code you write runs on 10,000+ assets across five continents. The hardware you design sits in motor control cabinets from the North Sea to the Australian outback. You see the result in production, not in a backlog.

Small enough to matter

At approximately 103 people, you're close to the decisions and close to the customers. Engineers talk to reliability teams. Data scientists visit sites. No layers between what you build and who it serves.

Supportive, not soft

Honest feedback. High standards. A team that has your back. The culture is fun and direct. We care about the work and about each other.

View Open Roles
